data.title: OFA Group clarifies their non-custodial Hearth platform in architecture, tech, asset mgmt. publicly traded on NASDAQ: OFAL.
data.description: Los Angeles, California – On January 26, 2026, OFA Group (NASDAQ: OFAL) provided vital information about its Hearth platform. The company, which specializes in architecture, technology, and asset management, clarified details about the platform's scope, functionality, and regulatory positioning.

data.title: Stablecoin market down, investors turning to gold not Bitcoin. BTC dropped 30% post-crash, while gold and silver hit new highs.
data.description: The value of Bitcoin has dropped by around 30% following a significant market crash in October. In contrast, the prices of gold and silver have seen a considerable increase, reaching record highs.

data.title: U.S. Army awards Salesforce a $5.6B contract over 10 years to boost military modernization, war readiness.
data.description: In a joint announcement from San Francisco and Washington, Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the leading AI CRM company, revealed that it has secured a significant contract with the U.S. Army. The contract, valued at $5.6 billion, spans a duration of 10 years and falls under the category of Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ). This landmark agreement was facilitated through Computable Insights LLC., a subsidiary exclusively catering to Salesforce's initiatives in national security. Salesforce's Missionforce National Security program will leverage cutting-edge innovations from the private sector to bolster the support provided to the U.S. Army.

data.title: PEL's appeal against Jura is dismissed by the UK Court in favor of Jura's subsidiaries, Spud and FHL, in Arbitration case.
data.description: In Calgary, Alberta, on January 26, 2026, Jura Energy Corporation announced that the UK Court has rejected the appeal by Petroleum Exploration (Private) Limited challenging the arbitrator’s jurisdiction over reliefs granted to Jura’s subsidiaries, Spud Energy (Pty) Limited and Frontier Holdings Limited. The dispute centered around the Settlement Agreement arbitral award. This decision favored Jura, Spud, and FHL as the appeal was fully dismissed by the UK Court. This legal outcome marks a significant development for Jura and its subsidiaries.

data.title: ANTA Sports to buy 29% of PUMA for EUR 1.5 billion to enhance global strategy.
data.description: In Hong Kong, ANTA Sports Products Limited disclosed a share purchase agreement with Groupe Artémis to obtain a 29.06% stake in PUMA SE, the renowned sports brand company, for EUR 1.5 billion. The agreement involves ANTA Sports acquiring the stake from the investment firm of the Pinault family. ANTA Sports, with stock codes 2020 (HKD counter) and 82020 (RMB counter), and its subsidiaries collectively referred to as the Group, view this transaction as a major advancement.

data.title: Hays County jury awards $46 million in wrongful death case of oil field worker run over by truck driver. Walker liable.
data.description: In San Marcos, Texas, a Hays County jury recently reached a $46 million verdict in favor of Andrew West's parents. West, an oil field worker, tragically lost his life in September 2019 when he was fatally run over by a truck driver at a well site in DeWitt County. The jury found truck driver Gerald Walker and John Dotson Trucking responsible for negligence leading to West's death. Walker was deemed 90% liable by the jury, with the remaining percentage of responsibility assigned to the company. Video footage featuring attorney comments regarding the case is available for reference.

data.title: Kalshi opens new office in DC, hires John Bivona as Head of Govt Relations to boost financial market innovation.
data.description: Kalshi, a US federally regulated exchange, has revealed plans to broaden its government relations endeavors by inaugurating a new office in Washington, DC. In line with this initiative, the company has appointed John Bivona as its inaugural Head of Federal Government Relations. Bivona, a seasoned political strategist and advisor with almost twenty years of experience, will spearhead efforts to advocate for responsible innovation within financial markets. His extensive background includes roles in various aspects of political campaigns and the federal government. This strategic move signifies Kalshi’s commitment to engaging with key stakeholders and promoting progressive developments within the industry.

data.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities reports in 4Q25 net loss of $1,081.8M, FFO $368.5M; 2025 net loss $1,438M, FFO $1,534.7M. Strong leasing, profitable balance sheet with $20.75B market cap and $5.30B in liquidity as of Dec 31, 2025. Dividend strategy includes a 45% reduction from $1.32 to $0.72 per share for 4Q25, maintaining financial strength and providing $410M annual basis support for 2026 capital plan. Plans sell of assets in 2026. 4Q25 leasing increased with rates fluctuating; 82% activity from existing tenant base. Expenses efficiently monitored to show 30% cost reduction, with approx. $51.3M saved in 2025. Plans for $300M reduction in construction costs over next few years. 2026 projection clarified based on market conditions, targeting $6.25-$6.55 FFO, maintaining strong capital metrics. Total planned investments $1.5-2B allocated to construction while reducing debt and ensuring operations sustainably. Expansions expected to bring $97M in new operating income by end of 2026. Strategic leasing, management cost-cutting, and investment gains noted to be critical for continued financial growth.
data.description: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. announced financial results for the fourth quarter and year-end December 31, 2025. The company reported a net loss of $1.4 billion for 2025, with a per-share loss of $8.44. Funds from operations for the year were approximately $1.53 billion, with a per-share value of $9.01. The company's occupancy rate was at 90.9% in North America, with 78% of rental revenue from Megacampus platform. The company has a total market capitalization of $20.75 billion and a dividend yield of 5.9%. Alexandria executed $1.81 billion in dispositions in 2025, with plans to sell additional assets in 2026. They also reduced general and administrative expenses by $51.3 million in 2025 and anticipate cost savings to continue in 2026. The company has a capital recycling strategy and a common stock repurchase program up to $500 million. Alexandria reported $1.5 billion in non-real estate investments and realized gains of $133.4 million. The company provided guidance for 2026 with a range of key credit metrics and assumptions. They expect reduction in debt, net cash from operations, and dispositions to align with construction needs. The company expects a decline in net operating income due to lease expirations in early 2026. Additionally, they anticipate potential tenant wind-downs and increased general and administrative expenses in 2026. Alexandria also forecasts realized gains on non-real estate investments for 2026.

data.title: Nevada families seek K-12 school options during School Choice Week with over 250 schools hosting open houses.
data.description: Starting today in Nevada and nationwide, National School Choice Week kicks off to assist parents in selecting the ideal K-12 schools for their children. Over 250 schools in Nevada are participating by organizing open houses as part of this initiative. This event is aimed at promoting educational options and empowering parents to make informed decisions regarding their children's education.

data.title: Innovent receives fast track designation from FDA for IBI3003, a trispecific antibody for multiple myeloma. Jan. 26, 2026.
data.description: On January 26, 2026, Innovent Biologics, Inc. (HKEX: 01801), a leading biopharmaceutical firm based in San Francisco and Suzhou, China, specializes in the development, production, and distribution of top-tier medications. Their focus is on treating various conditions including oncologic, autoimmune, and cardiovascular diseases.
